brent.label: Brent Crude $/bbl
brent.change: +50.9% since Feb 27 — Brent ~$107 (Apr 2 early); post-Trump speech surge reverses peace dip; Apr 1 close ~$104.86; Apr 6 power plant deadline 4 days away
brent.tooltip: Brent Crude: The international benchmark. Pre-war close: $71.00/bbl (Feb 27). Apr 1 close: ~$104.86/bbl. Apr 2 early trading: ~$107.15/bbl — surging after Trump's escalatory prime-time address offered no exit strategy and promised 2-3 more weeks of 'extremely hard' strikes. March +55% — largest monthly gain since inception in 1988. Goldman: $110 avg through March-April, settling to $71 Q4 if crisis resolves. Apr 6 energy strike deadline 4 days away. Source: ICE Futures Europe / Bloomberg / Goldman Sachs.

wti.label: WTI Crude $/bbl
wti.change: +58.2% since Feb 27 — WTI ~$105 (Apr 2 early); post-speech surge; some sources showing spikes to $112; Apr 1 close ~$100.12
wti.tooltip: West Texas Intermediate: The US benchmark. Pre-war close: $66.50/bbl (Feb 27). Apr 1 close: ~$100.12/bbl. Apr 2 early trading: ~$105.20/bbl — surging post-Trump speech with some sources showing intraday spikes to $112. Gas at $4.02-4.08/gal nationally. Diesel $5.49/gal. California ~$5.89/gal; CA diesel all-time high $7.17/gal. Goldman: every $10/bbl adds ~$0.25/gal at the pump. Apr 6 energy strike deadline 4 days away. Source: NYMEX / AAA / Bloomberg / Goldman Sachs.

gas.label: US Gas Avg/Gal
gas.change: +$1.07 / +35.9% since Feb 27 — $4.02-4.08 nationally (Apr 2) — largest monthly jump on record — CA ~$5.89 — CA diesel all-time high $7.17 — first >$4 since Aug 2022
gas.tooltip: National average retail gasoline price. Pre-war: $2.98/gal (Feb 27). Apr 2: $4.02-$4.08/gal nationally — first time above $4 since August 2022. Monthly jump of $1.08 exceeds steepest climbs after Hurricane Katrina (2005) and Russia-Ukraine invasion (2022). California at $5.89/gal; California diesel hit all-time high of $7.17/gal. Goldman: every $10/bbl crude increase adds ~$0.25/gal at pump. 33% increase in 30 days from ~$3.00 pre-war. Source: AAA / EIA / Goldman Sachs.

sp500.label: S&P 500
sp500.change: -357 pts / -5.2% — S&P ~6,522 (Apr 2 early); Dow futures -670 overnight; Nasdaq -1.19%; VIX surges to 27.31; Asian markets sell off sharply
sp500.tooltip: S&P 500 index. Pre-war close: 6,878.88 (Feb 27). Apr 1 close: 6,575.32 (+0.72%). Apr 2 early trading: ~6,522 (-0.81%) — reversal after Trump's escalatory speech offered no exit strategy. Dow futures dropped 670 points overnight (~46,145). Nasdaq ~21,582 (-1.19%). VIX surged to 27.31 (+11.3%). Asian markets sold off: Nikkei -2.1%, Kospi -3.9%, Hang Seng -1%. 10Y Treasury 4.38% (rising on inflation fears). Gold ~$4,660 (-3.19% profit-taking). LMT $617.52 (+28% YTD). RTX $195.52 (+6.5% YTD). XOM ~$160 (-5.2%). CVX ~$197 (-4.6%). DAL ~$64, UAL ~$91. Source: NYSE / Bloomberg / CNBC.

daily_cost.label: Est. Daily US Cost
daily_cost.note: ~$1.0-1.5B/day — IDF 170 targets in 24 hrs — 82nd Airborne ~2,000 arrived Middle East — Bush CSG ETA Apr 10-12 — ~50,000 US troops in theater — Apr 6 deadline 4 days
daily_cost.tooltip: Estimated daily operational cost. Day 34: ~$1.0-1.5B/day. IDF reported hitting 170 targets in prior 24 hours. 15,000+ total targets struck per CENTCOM/Pentagon. 82nd Airborne 1st BCT (~2,000 paratroopers) arrived in Middle East as of Mar 30. Bush CSG departed Norfolk Mar 31 — ETA CENTCOM ~Apr 10-12. Total US forces in Middle East: ~50,000. WSJ reports plans for up to 10,000 additional ground troops. Pentagon preparing 'weeks of ground operations.' Apr 6 energy deadline 4 days away. Source: Pentagon / CSIS / CENTCOM / Reuters / WSJ.

total_cost.label: Est. Total Cost (34 Days)
total_cost.note: Pentagon $11.3B Day 6 + CSIS $16.5B Day 12 + ~$1-1.5B/day ongoing — ~$40.6B — Penn Wharton: $40-95B direct, up to $210B total economic impact — $200B supplemental faces bipartisan opposition — 71% oppose
total_cost.tooltip: Cumulative estimated cost since Feb 28. Pentagon: $11.3B through Day 6. CSIS: $16.5B through Day 12. Extrapolated through Day 34: ~$40.55B. Penn Wharton Budget Model: $40B-$95B in direct costs, up to $210B total economic impact. IranWarCost.com tracker: ~$37B incrementing ~$200M/day. $200B supplemental not yet formally sent to Congress; 71% oppose (CNN poll Apr 1). Bipartisan opposition building. IEA: 32 member states released 400M barrels from strategic reserves including 172M from US SPR. Source: Pentagon / CSIS / Penn Wharton / CNN.

targets_struck.label: Targets Struck
targets_struck.note: CENTCOM: 15,000+ targets struck — IDF: 170 targets in 24 hrs — century-old medical research center Tehran — bridge, steel plants, pharmaceutical facilities — Bandar Abbas pier — Qeshm desalination
targets_struck.tooltip: Total targets struck by US and Israeli forces since Feb 28. CENTCOM/Pentagon: 15,000+ total targets struck. IDF reported hitting 170 targets in prior 24 hours including weapons research and production sites. Day 34 strikes: century-old medical research center in Tehran, bridge near Tehran, steel plants, pharmaceutical facilities, Bandar Abbas passenger pier, Qeshm Island desalination plant. CNN geolocated large explosions near Baharestan ~13 mi south of Isfahan. Combined US/Israeli extrapolated ~33,000+ through Day 34. 850+ Tomahawk cruise missiles fired (most in any single campaign per CSIS). Source: CENTCOM / IDF / Pentagon / CNN / CSIS.

us_kia.label: US Military Deaths
us_kia.note: 13-15 US KIA — Trump speech: 13 KIA; CENTCOM: 15 including non-combat — 303-520+ WIA — The Intercept: Pentagon 'casualty cover-up' — ~750 troops wounded or killed since Oct 2023
us_kia.tooltip: US military deaths. 13-15 KIA depending on source: Trump cited 13 in April 1 speech; CENTCOM reports 15 including non-combat deaths. Wounded: 303 per CENTCOM, potentially 520+ per The Intercept which reports Pentagon engaged in 'casualty cover-up.' ~750 troops wounded or killed across broader Middle East theater since October 2023. Key incidents: 6 soldiers killed at Camp Arifjan Kuwait (Mar 1); 6 airmen killed in KC-135 crash western Iraq (Mar 12). Source: CENTCOM / Pentagon / The Intercept / Stars and Stripes.

us_wia.label: US Service Members WIA
us_wia.note: 303-520+ WIA (CENTCOM vs. The Intercept) — 315 RTD ~91% — 82nd Airborne arrived — Bush CSG ETA Apr 10-12 — Tripoli operational — ~50,000 US troops in theater
us_wia.tooltip: US service members wounded/injured across all theaters since Feb 28. CENTCOM: 303 WIA; The Intercept: potentially 520+. Pentagon Mar 31 briefing: 348 WIA (315 returned to duty ~91%; 6 remain seriously wounded). 82nd Airborne 1st BCT (~2,000 paratroopers) arrived Middle East as of Mar 30 (Reuters). Tripoli ARG/31st MEU (~3,500) operational. Boxer ARG en route (ETA mid-to-late April). Bush CSG departed Norfolk Mar 31 (ETA ~Apr 10-12). Total US forces ~50,000; WSJ: up to 10,000 more planned. Source: CENTCOM / Pentagon / The Intercept / Reuters / USNI.

iranian_killed.label: Iranian Killed (est.)
iranian_killed.note: Health Min: 1,937+ killed, 24,800+ wounded — HRANA: 3,461+ (1,551 civilians, 236 children) — Iran Int'l: 4,700+ security forces — 83,351 buildings damaged — 3.2M displaced
iranian_killed.tooltip: Iranian casualties — dramatic discrepancies across sources reflecting fog of war and 20+ day internet blackout. Iranian Health Ministry: 1,937+ killed, 24,800+ wounded. HRANA independently documented 3,461+ deaths including 1,551 civilians. Iran International: 4,700+ security forces killed. 83,351 residential buildings damaged. 65+ schools, 77+ healthcare facilities (31 major clinical, 12 inactive). 120+ historical/cultural heritage sites including UNESCO-listed Golestan Palace. Schools ordered closed; Tehran described as 'ghost town.' 3.2 million displaced. Internet at 4% of normal (NetBlocks) — longest blackout in Iran's history (20+ days). Source: Iran Health Ministry / HRANA / Iran International / OCHA / NetBlocks.

lebanese_killed.label: Lebanese Killed
lebanese_killed.note: 1,200+ killed — 3,119+ wounded — over 1M displaced (~20% of pop.) — 180,000+ crossed into Syria — Hezbollah Southern Front cmdr Hashem killed — IDF expanding buffer zone to Litani
lebanese_killed.tooltip: Lebanese casualties from Israeli strikes and ground invasion since March 2. 1,200+ killed per Lebanese MoH. 3,119+ wounded. Over 1 million displaced (~20-25% of population), with 180,000+ crossing into Syria. Day 33-34: Hezbollah fired 50+ rockets at northern Israel (2 lightly wounded). IDF expanding 'security buffer zone' to Litani River. Hezbollah Southern Front cmdr Hashem killed in Beirut strike (Apr 1). Full Israeli ground operations launched March 16. 472 schools used as shelters. Source: Lebanon Health Ministry / IDF / UNHCR / WHO.

displaced.label: Displaced Persons
displaced.note: 4.4M+ displaced — Iran 3.2M — Lebanon 830K-1.2M+ — 180,000+ Lebanese crossed into Syria — ~2,000 vessels and ~20,000 seafarers stranded
displaced.tooltip: People displaced from their homes across all fronts. 4.4M+ total displaced. Iran: ~3.2 million internally displaced per UNHCR. Lebanon: 830,000-1.2 million (~20-25% of population), with 180,000+ crossing into Syria. Israel: ~4,900 displaced. Additionally ~2,000 vessels and ~20,000 seafarers remain stranded due to Hormuz closure. Global humanitarian aid disrupted. Dubai's International Humanitarian City effectively crippled. OCHA activated contingency plans across 7 countries. Source: UNHCR / OCHA / WaPo / IMO.

flights_cancelled.label: Flights Cancelled
flights_cancelled.note: 52,000+ flights — ~2,000 vessels stranded — ~20,000 seafarers waiting — insurance 5-10% of vessel value — first LNG tanker (Sohar LNG) attempting transit since war began
flights_cancelled.tooltip: Commercial flights cancelled due to airspace closures: 52,000+ since Feb 28. ~2,000 vessels and ~20,000 seafarers stranded. Insurance rates surged from 0.125-0.25% to 5-10% of hull value per transit. Iran 'tollbooth' system at Larak Island charging up to $2M per crossing in Chinese yuan or crypto. Day 34: First LNG tanker (Omani-flagged Sohar LNG) attempting transit since war began (Bloomberg). 71% of all transiting ships since Mar 1 are Iran-linked. Mar 1-25: only 142 total transits — down 93% (USNI/Lloyd's). Source: IMO / IATA / Lloyd's / Bloomberg / Windward.

children_killed.label: Children Killed
children_killed.note: HRANA: 236+ children (Iran) + Lebanon 107+ + Israel 4 + Kuwait 1 — IRGC cluster bomb children wounded in Bnei Brak — 600+ schools attacked — Minab school 165+ killed
children_killed.tooltip: Children killed in the conflict. HRANA: 236+ children killed in Iran. Lebanon: 107+ children. Israel: 4. Kuwait: 1. Day 34: IRGC cluster-warhead missiles continue targeting Israeli cities — 4 civilians including 2 infants wounded in Bnei Brak. Minab school (Feb 28): 165-170 killed, mostly girls aged 7-12. 600+ educational centers attacked in Iran per Red Crescent. 65+ schools destroyed. Bushehr NPP struck 3 times — nearest impact 350m from reactor holding 72 tons fuel + 210 tons spent fuel. Source: UNICEF / HRANA / Lebanese MoH / UNHRC / ICRC.
